task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully logged in using the provided credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS home page or administration dashboard is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the concept dictionary management area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the OpenMRS Dictionary or 'View/Edit Concepts' page.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent searched for and located the target concept.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search field contains 'Legacy Pain Management' and the concept appears in the search results or is opened.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent entered the correct reason for retiring the concept.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text input field for 'Retire Reason' contains the text 'Replaced by 2026 Guidelines'.

task_completion.description: Verify the concept was retired (not purged or deleted).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message confirming the concept was retired, or the concept details page showing its status as 'Retired' (often indicated by a checkbox, status label, or strikethrough) without being completely removed from the system.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web browser GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser interacting with the OpenMRS interface, not terminal commands (like curl or mysql).

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the live application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows the correct local OpenMRS address (e.g., localhost/openmrs) and the interface responds to inputs.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply edit the HTML using browser developer tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open and being used to fake the 'Retired' status or reason.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Concept Name: 'Legacy Pain Management'. Required Retire Reason: Must contain '2026 Guidelines'. The OpenMRS administration panel is located at http://localhost/openmrs/admin/index.htm.
